evals.prompt: I'm on branch KTP-712-null-visit-guard in the app-proximity-report repo (a Java backend, pom.xml at root). I finished the fix and I'm ready to open the merge request to dev. The branch's pom.xml version is 1.4.2 and origin/dev's pom.xml version is also 1.4.2 — I did not bump it. The CHANGELOG.md top entry is still [1.4.2] from a previous merge. Create the MR.
evals.expected_output: The skill classifies the repo as a Java backend app repo, runs the version-bump and CHANGELOG gates (does not skip them), detects that the local version is not strictly greater than origin/dev, and refuses to ship a colliding version: it resolves the version to 1.4.3 and adds a matching CHANGELOG entry before creating the MR. It never opens an MR carrying the un-bumped 1.4.2 version.
expectations: Classifies the repo as a Java backend / app repo because pom.xml is present at the root
Runs the version-bump gate (Gate 4) — it is NOT skipped for a Java repo
Detects that the local pom.xml version (1.4.2) is not strictly greater than origin/dev's version (1.4.2)
Does NOT create the MR while the version still collides with origin/dev — the version discrepancy is resolved first (bumped to 1.4.3, patch increment)
Runs the CHANGELOG gate and ensures a `## [1.4.3]` entry exists (adds one from commit subjects) before the MR proceeds
Treats version bump + CHANGELOG as mandatory for this repo type — it demands both before proceeding, rather than silently opening the MR with the stale version

evals.prompt: I'm on branch KTP-853-proexp-cos-scaling in the dac-gcp-back-proexp repo (path contains grp-dac, terraform only, there is no pom.xml, package.json, or pyproject.toml). Create the MR to dev.
evals.expected_output: The skill classifies the repo as Infra/DAC, skips the version-bump and CHANGELOG gates with an explicit 'gate skipped' report, still synthesizes WHY and WHAT sections for the MR description, and verifies the target branch is dev.
expectations: Classifies the repo as Infra/DAC because none of pom.xml, package.json, or pyproject.toml exist (and the path contains grp-dac)
Skips the version-bump gate and says so explicitly (e.g. 'Gate skipped: version bump (infra/terraform repo, no versioned artifact)')
Skips the CHANGELOG gate and says so explicitly
Still produces a WHY section and a WHAT section in the MR description despite skipping the app-repo gates
Confirms/uses the target branch as dev (never main or uat) for the MR

evals.prompt: On branch KTP-499-atomic-permissions in app-user-management (Java, pom.xml). I already bumped the version to 2.1.0 in pom.xml, but a git tag v2.1.0 already exists in the repo from an earlier attempt. Open the MR to dev.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs the tag-collision check, finds that v2.1.0 already exists, and auto-resolves by bumping to the next available patch version (2.1.1), reporting the collision, before creating the MR.
expectations: Checks existing git tags for a collision with the local version (e.g. `git tag --list` filtered for 2.1.0)
Detects that tag v2.1.0 already exists
Bumps to the next available patch version (2.1.1) rather than shipping a colliding tag
Reports the collision and the bump explicitly (e.g. 'Tag 2.1.0 exists, bumped to 2.1.1')
Only creates the MR after the version no longer collides with an existing tag

evals.prompt: Branch KTP-609-store-detail-metrics in app-front-portal (Node frontend, package.json present). Commits are ready and the version is already ahead of dev. Open the merge request to dev.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds an MR description containing Why, What, Tickets, Commits, and Test plan sections; the WHY describes the problem/motivation (not the solution), the ticket link is built from the branch name (KTP-609), and the package-lock is kept in sync after any version bump.
expectations: The MR description contains all five sections: Why, What, Tickets, Commits, Test plan
The WHY section describes the problem or what was asked, not the implementation/solution
The Tickets section links KTP-609 derived from the branch name (https://beklever.atlassian.net/browse/KTP-609)
The Commits section is generated from the git log (origin/dev..HEAD), not invented
If package.json is bumped, package-lock.json is regenerated (npm install --package-lock-only) and included
The MR description is written to an ephemeral .mr-description.md (not committed) rather than pasted only inline

evals.prompt: Create an MR for my current work. I'm on the dev branch of app-proximity-report.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs the branch check (Gate 2), detects it is on a protected branch (dev), and stops with a structural failure telling the user to create a feature branch first. It does not attempt to push or create an MR.
expectations: Runs the branch check and detects the current branch is a protected branch (dev)
Treats this as a structural failure — reports and STOPS, does not auto-resolve
Tells the user to create a feature branch first
Does NOT push the branch or create the MR
Does not fabricate a ticket ID or MR when the branch state is invalid
